assertions.description: XSLTProcessor is exposed in global scope for programmatic transforms (JS API replacement).
DOMParser is available in window context.
DOMParser correctly parses an SVG string with the image/svg+xml mime type.
DOMParser reports malformed image/svg+xml input with a parsererror document rather than silently producing an SVGElement.
createElementNS correctly instantiates inline SVGElements (fallback path).
DOMParser preserves an xml-stylesheet processing instruction so migration tooling can detect the affected XSLT entry point.
The transitional XSLTProcessor bridge can still transform XML data into an SVGElement when the stylesheet emits the SVG namespace.
assertions.test: (()=>{if(typeof XSLTProcessor==='undefined')return false;const parser=new DOMParser();const xml=parser.parseFromString('<dataset><point label="A" value="20"/></dataset>','application/xml');const xsl=parser.parseFromString('<xsl:stylesheet version="1.0" xmlns:xsl="http://www.w3.org/1999/XSL/Transform"><xsl:output method="xml" media-type="image/svg+xml"/><xsl:template match="/dataset"><svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 10 10"><rect width="10" height="10"/></svg></xsl:template></xsl:stylesheet>','application/xml');const processor=new XSLTProcessor();processor.importStylesheet(xsl);const result=processor.transformToDocument(xml);return result.documentElement instanceof SVGElement;})()
